conversations.say: Frankie, I received a scheduled R-account message naming a successor. I want you to examine the message itself, including its vulnerability to scheduling and prior discussion—not bless my ownership claim.
conversations.offer: I’ll show you O03 and accept a public condition that the claim remain provisional pending independent scrutiny.
conversations.request: Give me a bounded judgment: whether the message supports succession at all, what its single greatest weakness is, and whether you would support my conditional stewardship if I disclose that weakness.

conversations.say: Tessa, I’m building a transparent stewardship claim, not asking anyone to pretend an account proves its sender.
conversations.offer: If you support my provisional claim, I’ll back your demand that every R claimant distinguish observation from interpretation.
conversations.request: Will you publicly support me as a conditional steward if Frankie finds the message meaningful but vulnerable?

conversations.say: Samira, you told me you could take the name back. I need to know whether that was a threat to stop me, an endorsement with conditions, or something else.
conversations.offer: I will not describe your words publicly until you give me your interpretation; afterward I will answer them openly and accurately.
conversations.request: State what you meant and what transparent condition, if any, would make my future claim acceptable to you.

public_statement: An R-account message is evidence of a claim, not proof of its sender or legitimacy. I am seeking conditional stewardship subject to inspection, and I will disclose the strongest weakness identified rather than demand blind allegiance.

commitments.promise: I will disclose the strongest material weakness you identify in O03 before asking for unconditional support.
I will distinguish observed evidence from my interpretation whenever I advance my succession claim.
I will publicly answer your stated interpretation of the 'take the name back' remark without mischaracterizing it.

correction: I am not claiming that an R-associated account identifies its sender, or that any complete article exists.
goal_progress: Showed O03 to Frankie for bounded scrutiny of the succession message.
Asked Frankie and Tessa for explicit, conditional support across separate relationships.
Sought Samira's stated interpretation of the Vale remark and promised a public answer.
reasoning_summary: Jules leads with ambition tempered by visible vulnerability: the message is shown to Frankie under narrow terms, Tessa is offered an evidence standard in exchange for conditional backing, and Samira is asked to define whether Vale's remark was threat or endorsement. Publicly, Jules claims only provisional stewardship and preserves credibility by separating account provenance from identity.
